title: Settings screen — Server and Cloudflare Access sections
description: Implement SettingsView.swift as a Form with two sections. Section 'Server': TextField bound to credentialsStore.baseURL (.keyboardType(.URL), .autocorrectionDisabled()); Button 'Test Connection' that calls healthCheck() and shows inline status below — success: Label('Connected · v{version}', systemImage: 'checkmark.circle.fill') tinted .green; failure: Label('{error}', systemImage: 'exclamationmark.triangle.fill') tinted .red; in-flight: inline ProgressView. Section 'Cloudflare Access': TextField for clientId (.autocorrectionDisabled(), .textInputAutocapitalization(.never)); SecureField for clientSecret. No Save button — CredentialsStore persists on didSet.
acceptance_criteria: Form uses default .insetGrouped style (no explicit .listStyle needed on iOS 26)
Section 'Server' contains Base URL TextField and Test Connection button
Section 'Cloudflare Access' contains Client ID TextField and Client Secret SecureField
Test Connection shows inline ProgressView while in-flight
Test Connection shows green checkmark label with version string on success
Test Connection shows red triangle label with error message on failure
No Save button is present anywhere in the form
